title: Veeranarayana Temple, Gadag
tagline: Veeranarayana Temple, Gadag — spiritual town of Karnataka

overview.short: Veeranarayana Temple in Gadag city is a Hindu temple known to have been built around 11th century by the Chalukyas of Kalyani.
overview.description: Veeranarayana Temple in Gadag city is a Hindu temple known to have been built around 11th century by the Chalukyas of Kalyani. Gadag city is the administrative headquarters of Gadag district in the state of Karnataka, India. The primary deity in temple is the Hindu god Narayana (also known as Vishnu). The Veeranarayana temple is a protected monument under the Karnataka state division of the Archaeological Survey of India.

topPlaces.name: Gadag-Betageri
topPlaces.description: Gadaga-Betageri is a city municipal council in Gadag district in the state of Karnataka, India. It is the administrative headquarters of Gadag District.

topPlaces.name: Trikuteshwara Temple, Gadag
topPlaces.description: Trikuteshwara temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to Shiva. The carved temple is in the town of Gadag, 50 km southeast of Hubli-Dharwad, in Karnataka, India.

topPlaces.name: Tontadarya Matha
topPlaces.description: Shree Jagadguru Tontadarya Samsthana Math is a Lingayata monastery located in the heart of Gadag city of Karnataka. It has a rich history and tradition, and is engaged in many educational and literary activities in and around Gadag.

topPlaces.name: Kappatagudda Wildlife Sanctuary
topPlaces.description: Kappatagudda Wildlife Sanctuary is a wildlife sanctuary located in Gadag district in the Indian state of Karnataka. It was formed in 2019 and it spans 244.15 km2 (94.27 sq mi) over dry deciduous hills across Gadag, Mundargi, and Shirahatti taluks.

topPlaces.name: Adavisomapur, Gadag
topPlaces.description: Adavisomapur (Gadag) is a village in the southern state of Karnataka, India. It is located in the Gadag taluk of Gadag district in Karnataka.

topPlaces.name: Hulkoti
topPlaces.description: Hulkoti is a town in Gadag district of Karnataka state, India. The town is situated on the way from Hubli to Gadag road(NH-63).The town is known for the co-operative movement in Karnataka.K.H. Patil was in the forefront of co-operative movement in this town.

topPlaces.name: Papanashi
topPlaces.description: Papanashi is a village in the Gadag district of the Indian state of Karnataka. Papanashi is famous for the ancient Kalmeshwara Temple located in the village.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Veeranarayana Temple, Gadag?
faq.a: The best time to visit Veeranarayana Temple, Gadag is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Veeranarayana Temple, Gadag?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Hubli Airport (57 km) and the nearest railway station is Hubballi Junction (51 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Veeranarayana Temple, Gadag?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Veeranarayana Temple, Gadag at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Veeranarayana Temple, Gadag?
faq.a: Stays in Veeranarayana Temple, Gadag start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Veeranarayana Temple, Gadag?
faq.a: The top attractions are Gadag-Betageri, Trikuteshwara Temple, Gadag, Tontadarya Matha, Kappatagudda Wildlife Sanctuary, Adavisomapur, Gadag.
